responseMessageContents.text: The user provided a new API key starting with `vcp_` instead of `vck_`, which is unusual since Vercel AI Gateway keys typically use the `vck_` prefix. I should test this key directly against the ai-gateway.vercel.sh endpoint to see if it's valid.
Testing this key live right now.

responseMessageContents.inputArgsStr: {"command": "KEY=\"vcp_1UWzfu1up7xx9gRXsSZxjAsNzYdzcIEXUuf4wUPfpQCyCjPYOX1hjUKO\"; echo \"len=${#KEY} prefix=${KEY:0:4}\"; echo \"--- credits ---\"; curl -s -w \"\\nHTTP:%{http_code}\\n\" https://ai-gateway.vercel.sh/v1/credits -H \"Authorization: Bearer $KEY\" | head -c 500; echo; echo \"--- chat completion ---\"; curl -s -w \"\\nHTTP:%{http_code}\\n\" https://ai-gateway.vercel.sh/v1/chat/completions -H \"Authorization: Bearer $KEY\" -H \"Content-Type: application/json\" -d '{\"model\":\"openai/gpt-4o\",\"messages\":[{\"role\":\"user\",\"content\":\"Reply with exactly: IVX KEY OK\"}],\"max_tokens\":10}' | head -c 700"}

responseMessageContents.text: The bash shell being used is `sh` (dash), which doesn't support the substring expansion syntax I tried, so I need to use a simpler approach instead.

responseMessageContents.text:
The user's API key has a `vcp_` prefix, which isn't the right format for AI Gateway — those should start with `vck_`. I need to clarify that they're using the wrong key type, possibly a Vercel provisioning or project key instead of an actual API Gateway key.

The error response from the credits endpoint actually includes a direct link to create a new key with the modal pre-opened, so I can share that URL with them as proof and a quick way to generate the correct key.

Tested live — this key is also rejected. Here's the proof:

```
Key: vcp_...UKO (60 chars)
→ https://ai-gateway.vercel.sh/v1/credits
HTTP 401 "Authentication failed. Create an API key and set in
AI_GATEWAY_API_KEY environment variable"
→ /v1/chat/completions
HTTP 401 "Authentication failed. Check that your Vercel credential
is valid and has access to AI Gateway."
```

**Why:** this key starts with `vcp_` — that's a different kind of Vercel credential (project/personal token). AI Gateway keys **always start with `vck_`**. Vercel itself is telling us in the error to create a proper AI Gateway key.

## Do exactly this — Vercel's own shortcut link

Open this URL on your phone (it jumps straight to the Create Key popup):

**https://vercel.com/d?to=%2F%5Bteam%5D%2F~%2Fai-gateway%2Fapi-keys%3FshowCreateKeyModal**

1. Log in if asked, pick your team
2. The **"Create API Key"** popup opens automatically → name it `ivx-backend` → **Create**
3. Copy the key — it must start with **`vck_`**
4. Paste it here, and I'll verify it live + bind it to Render for you (also paste your full ~35-char Render API key if you want me to do the Render step too)

You were one prefix away — the page you're on is right, just make sure the key you copy begins with `vck_`, not `vcp_`.

responseMessageContents.title: Updating the app setup and fixing the chat assistant connection.
responseMessageContents.description: We moved the app's code to your personal GitHub account to ensure you have full control. We also adjusted the settings to make sure the app runs correctly as a mobile application and began fixing the chat feature so it can securely communicate with the AI service using the correct security keys.
